macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Futures Fall as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz, Oil Prices Spike
items.summary: Futures tied to the S&P 500 and Nasdaq declined on Monday amid escalating US-Iran hostilities in the Gulf, which drove oil prices to a one-month high and rattled chip stocks.

items.title: Fed Governor Waller Says Rates May Need to Rise 'In the Near Term' if Inflation Stays High
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller warned on Monday that the central bank may need to raise interest rates soon if data confirms inflation remains well above the 2% target, calling policy 'at a crossroads.'

items.title: India's Retail Inflation Accelerates to 4.4% in June, Breaching RBI Target
items.summary: India's retail inflation rose to 4.38–4.4% in June, surpassing the central bank's 4% target for the first time in 17 months due to higher food and transport costs, setting the stage for a potential rate-hiking cycle.

items.title: June US Federal Budget Deficit Swells to $120B Due to Tariff Refunds
items.summary: The US Treasury reported the June federal budget deficit jumped to $120 billion, a sharp reversal from the $27 billion surplus in June 2025, driven by refunds from President Trump's illegal tariffs.

items.title: Micron, SK Hynix, and Sandisk Dive as Chip Stocks Face Volatility on AI Capex Questions
items.summary: Major chip stocks including Micron, SK Hynix, and Sandisk fell sharply on Monday as investors grapple with high valuations and uncertainty over the longevity of the AI capital expenditure boom.

items.title: Singapore Q2 GDP Rises 5.7% Driven by AI Demand; South Korea Forecasts 5-Year High Growth
items.summary: Singapore's preliminary Q2 GDP grew 5.7% year-over-year fueled by AI-related demand, while South Korea forecasts 2026 economic growth at a five-year high due to its AI chip boom.

items.title: SpaceX Stock Plunges to All-Time Low After Competitor Leap; Starship Test Flight Set
items.summary: SpaceX shares hit an all-time low following a major competitor's technological leap, though the company is preparing for Starship's 13th test flight later this week.

items.title: China Implements Revised Trading Rules to Expand After-Hours Trading on Major Exchanges
items.summary: China's Shanghai, Shenzhen, and Beijing stock exchanges enacted revised trading rules on Monday to expand after-hours trading, refine pricing mechanisms, and improve market liquidity.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops 3.13% to $60,775 Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin declined by 3.13% over the past 24 hours, settling at $60,775, while Ethereum traded around $1,785 as total crypto market capitalization eased to $2.15 trillion[2][4].

items.title: U.S. Spot Bitcoin ETFs End 8 Weeks of Outflows with $197.4M Inflows
items.summary: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $197.4M in net inflows the week ending July 10, ending eight straight weeks of outflows, though analysts urge caution on recovery calls[6].

items.title: Major U.S. Banks Launch Tokenized Deposit Network to Counter Stablecoin Growth
items.summary: Major U.S. banks launched a tokenized deposit network to counter stablecoin growth, a development that could reshape adoption dynamics across DeFi, DEXs, and centralized exchanges[4].

items.title: Uniswap Community Votes 74% to Support Ongoing UNI Burn Plan
items.summary: The Uniswap community vote shows 74% backing for a governance package creating the protocol's first ongoing UNI burn mechanism funded by protocol fees[5].

items.title: Solana DEX Jupiter Launches Beta 'Jupiter Gacha' for On-Chain Pokémon Cards
items.summary: Solana DEX Jupiter has launched Jupiter Gacha, a beta platform letting collectors buy real, graded Pokémon and One Piece cards that are fully on-chain and tradable[6].

items.title: Thailand Central Bank Audits High-Volume USDT Transactions in Crackdown on Illicit Finance
items.summary: Thailand's central bank is auditing high-volume USDT transactions and cash flows as part of a broader crackdown on gray money and scam-linked illicit finance[6].

items.title: Hong Kong SFC Orders Crypto Platforms to Replace OTP Logins with Phishing-Resistant Authentication
items.summary: Hong Kong's SFC ordered crypto platforms and online brokers to replace OTP logins with phishing-resistant authentication within 12 months as spoofing attacks hit 57% of 2025 incidents[6].

items.title: Jito DAO Approves Buyback Plan for JTO Holders Through 2027
items.summary: Jito DAO proposed directing protocol revenue toward JTO holders and using all JTX income for automatic JTO buybacks and token burns through 2027[8].

items.title: Base Network Shifts Strategy Away from Content Coins to Focus on Infrastructure and AI
items.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong acknowledged that Base's content coin strategy is a failure; the network will now concentrate on exchanging infrastructure, payments, and AI agents[8].

items.title: Pi Network Price Falls Over 16% to $0.07 USDT in 24 Hours
items.summary: Pi Network's price fell from nearly $0.10 USDT to $0.07 USDT, a drop of over 16% in 24 hours, causing significant value loss for the token[11].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Live Voice Models and Restricts GPT-5.6 Sol Amid US Government Review
items.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-Live, a new family of real-time voice models capable of simultaneous listening and speaking, while restricting access to its GPT-5.6 Sol model at the request of President Trump's administration for cybersecurity review.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic File for IPOs Targeting $1T and $965B Valuations
items.summary: Both frontier AI labs filed for initial public offerings with unprecedented target valuations, despite intensified regulatory scrutiny and a coordinated investigation by 42 US state attorneys general into OpenAI's data practices.

items.title: Meta Expands AI Infrastructure with 5GW Louisiana Data Center and New Muse Spark Models
items.summary: Meta announced its Louisiana data center will expand to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity and released two new AI models in its Muse Spark family, including Muse Spark 1.1 for agentic workloads.

items.title: Apple Ships Long-Delayed Siri AI in iOS 27 Public Beta with Conversational Capabilities
items.summary: Apple finally released Siri AI in the iOS 27 and macOS 27 public betas, transforming the assistant into a conversational agent with on-screen context awareness and multi-step in-app actions.

items.title: xAI Rebranded to SpaceXAI Releases Grok 4.5 Coding Model with Deep Cursor Integration
items.summary: The company formerly known as xAI, now rebranded as SpaceXAI, shipped Grok 4.5, an 'Opus-class' coding model priced at $2 per 1M input tokens with integrated support for the Cursor code editor.

items.title: China's New AI Regulations Target Emotional Dependence and Minors Ahead of July 15 Implementation
items.summary: New Chinese regulations targeting emotional dependence in AI and content unsuitable for minors will take effect July 15, prompting Alibaba to ban employee use of Claude Code and disable Qwen humanoid-agent features.

items.title: DeepSeek Raises $7B at $55B Valuation While Building Custom Inference Chips
items.summary: DeepSeek is raising $7 billion at a ~$55 billion valuation and developing its own inference chip, maintaining V4 Flash pricing an order of magnitude below GPT-5.6.

items.title: Intel Begins €5B Capital Investment in Ireland to Expand AI Manufacturing Output
items.summary: Intel launched a €5 billion ($5.7 billion) capital investment at its Irish campus to expand manufacturing output in Europe and meet growing global demand for AI and high-performance computing.

items.title: Mistral AI Raises €600M Series C at €8B Valuation to Fund EU Infrastructure
items.summary: Mistral AI secured €600 million in Series C funding at an €8 billion valuation to develop new models and expand European infrastructure, signaling confidence in competitive EU foundation models.

items.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba's Qwen Lab of Largest Known AI Distillation Heist
items.summary: Anthropic alleged that Alibaba's Qwen lab executed the largest known distillation heist, using ~25,000 fake accounts and 28.8 million Claude interactions to extract coding capabilities.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced launches worldwide, selling 2 million copies on day one
items.summary: Ubisoft's remastered pirate adventure hit 2 million sales and 100,000 concurrent players on launch day, though it faced criticism over microtransactions.

items.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved goes gold ahead of late-July release
items.summary: Halo Studios confirmed the game is feature-complete and locked for its scheduled late-July release without delays.

items.title: EA Sports College Football 27 launches worldwide on PS5, Xbox, PC, and mobile
items.summary: EA launched its first-ever College Football game on PC and mobile platforms alongside PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

items.title: Steam suffers major global outage on July 13, disrupting thousands of users
items.summary: Steam experienced a significant service outage on the evening of July 13, causing thousands of users worldwide to lose access temporarily.

items.title: Halo Studios reportedly shuts down Project Ekur multiplayer prototype after internal testing
items.summary: The studio canceled Project Ekur, a multiplayer Halo prototype, despite it having already passed internal testing phases.

items.title: Square Enix announces Octopath Traveler 1 & 2 native Switch 2 versions for October 1, 2026
items.summary: Native Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Octopath Traveler and its sequel will launch on October 1, 2026, with a bundle also available digitally today.

items.title: Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls launches August 6 on PC and PS5 with Episode Mode story
items.summary: The new Marvel fighting game includes a story mode written by Kieron Gillen and splits its narrative across multiple Marvel teams.

items.title: Games Done Quick cancels SNK stream due to Saudi Arabia ties
items.summary: The charity speedrunning organization abruptly canceled a planned SNK stream citing the company's ties with Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Nintendo releases free Yoshi and the Mysterious Book demo for Switch 2
items.summary: Nintendo launched a free demo for one of its top Switch 2 titles, allowing players to try Yoshi and the Mysterious Book.

items.title: Hundreds of layoffs confirmed at Elder Scrolls Online studio amid Xbox cuts
items.summary: Elder Scrolls Online studio confirmed hundreds of layoffs as Xbox continues its broader restructuring and cost-cutting measures.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft in Bombshell Lawsuit
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI and two former employees of stealing trade secrets and confidential hardware information to develop competing AI-powered devices, marking a dramatic fallout between the once-partner companies.

items.title: Bosch Begins Sample Production at First U.S. Semiconductor Factory
items.summary: German chip maker Bosch started sample production of silicon carbide chips at its first U.S. factory, finalizing a $225 million agreement with the U.S. Commerce Department to strengthen domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

items.title: Intel Launches €5 Billion Manufacturing Expansion in Ireland
items.summary: Intel initiated a €5 billion ($5.7 billion) capital investment at its Irish campus to expand manufacturing output for high-performance computing and meet global demand, upgrading facilities with leading-edge equipment.

items.title: Meta Plans $9.1 Billion AI Data Center in Canada, Largest Outside U.S.
items.summary: Meta announced it will invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first AI data center in Canada, which will be its largest facility outside the United States.

items.title: Global Smartphone Shipments Fall 11% in Q2 Due to Memory Chip Shortage
items.summary: Early estimates from Counterpoint Research show global smartphone shipments dropped 11% in the second quarter to their lowest level since 2013, driven by a prolonged memory chip shortage that raised handset prices.

items.title: Fiat Launching Tiny European EV in U.S. with Low Price Tag
items.summary: Fiat is preparing to launch a small European electric vehicle in the U.S. market at a low price point, though reports indicate there is a significant catch to the deal.

items.title: Shein Nears Market Debut After Hong Kong IPO Approval
items.summary: Fast-fashion retailer Shein received approval from Chinese regulators for its Hong Kong IPO, positioning the company for its imminent market debut.

items.title: LAPD Ends Contract with Surveillance Giant Flock Over Privacy Concerns
items.summary: The Los Angeles Police Department terminated its contract with surveillance company Flock, citing serious concerns over civil liberties and privacy after being one of Flock's biggest government customers.

items.title: Spain's Submer Group Invests $2 Billion in India's Semiconductor Sector
items.summary: Spain's Submer Group announced a $2 billion investment in Madhya Pradesh's semiconductor sector, expected to generate approximately 5,000 direct employment opportunities.

items.title: HMD Asha 505 Leaked with Lumia-Inspired Design and 5-Inch Display
items.summary: New leaked renders show the HMD Asha 505 surfacing online with a design inspired by classic Nokia Lumia phones and featuring a 5-inch display.
